data.body:
## Description

**What**: Create the `dso:red-test-writer` plugin agent definition at `plugins/dso/agents/red-test-writer.md`. This sonnet-tier agent receives atomic RED tasks (create-test, modify-existing-test) from implementation-plan and writes behavioral tests that execute code under test and assert on output, exit code, or side effects. It rejects change-detector patterns and produces structured diagnostic failure messages.

**Why**: Sub-agents currently produce change-detector tests (grepping source files for patterns) that pass TDD gates without verifying behavioral correctness. A dedicated agent with strong behavioral enforcement prevents this failure mode.

**Scope**:
- IN: Agent definition file, behavioral test enforcement prompt, change-detector rejection logic, structured failure message schema (JSON contract consumed by dso:red-test-evaluator in Story 2)
- OUT: Orchestrator integration (Stories 3, 4), evaluator agent (Story 2), prompt engineering details (to be brainstormed post-preplanning and folded into this story)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a `dso:red-test-writer` agent definition exists at `plugins/dso/agents/red-test-writer.md` with model tier sonnet
  ← Satisfies: "A dso:red-test-writer plugin agent (sonnet) that receives atomic RED tasks"
- When this story is complete, the agent definition includes explicit rejection criteria for change-detector patterns (grepping source files, checking file contents for string patterns)
  ← Satisfies: "The agent rejects change-detector patterns"
- When this story is complete, the agent definition specifies a structured failure message schema (JSON with required fields) that the dso:red-test-evaluator can parse reliably
  ← Satisfies: "produces a diagnostic failure message when the task spec is insufficient"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Agent definitions are prompt-based — behavioral tests must verify the agent produces correct output patterns for representative inputs, not just file existence
- [Interface] Define the exact failure message schema (JSON fields, required keys) in the agent definition so the evaluator in Story 2 can parse it reliably. Document this as an interface contract between the two agents
